_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 5-4 Adult Pulmonary Edema/Cardiogenic Shock (Revised 06/03/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Adult Cardiac Protocol Section 5-4: Pulmonary Edema/Cardiogenic Shock — MDHHS Approved 6/3/23, Reviewed 2023
purpose: Protocol for patients in respiratory distress due to pulmonary edema with or without hypotension (CHF/fluid overload or Cardiogenic Shock). Pulmonary edema usually presents with crackles — continuously evaluate as they may evolve with treatments.

sections.title: Pulmonary Edema / Cardiogenic Shock Treatment
points: Initiate supplemental oxygen by non-rebreather mask
Position patient upright with legs dependent if possible
Establish IV access without delaying treatment per Vascular Access & IV Fluid Therapy-Procedure Protocol
If WHEEZING: administer albuterol 2.5 mg/3 mL NS nebulized (per MCA selection may be EMT skill) per Medication Administration-Medication Protocol

If CRACKLES (with or without wheezing): administer nitroglycerin as outlined below — inquire of ALL patients regardless of identified gender if they have taken an erectile dysfunction medication or medications used to treat pulmonary hypertension in the last 48 hours
If ED/pulmonary hypertension medication taken in last 48 hours: DO NOT ADMINISTER NITROGLYCERIN — contact Medical Control
Prior to IV establishment, if no ED medication and SBP > 120 mmHg: administer nitroglycerin 0.4 mg SL up to maximum 3 doses
With IV in place, if SBP > 100 mmHg: administer nitroglycerin 0.4 mg SL — repeat every 3–5 minutes while SBP remains > 100 mmHg
If wheezing continues: continue nitroglycerin 0.4 mg SL and consider albuterol/ipratropium bromide per Respiratory Distress-Treatment Protocol

If SBP < 100 mmHg — TREAT FOR CARDIOGENIC SHOCK: push-dose epinephrine 10 mcg/mL (prepare by adding 1 mL of epinephrine 1 mg/10 mL in 9 mL NS); administer 20 mcg (2 mL) IV/IO; repeat every 3–5 minutes; titrate SBP > 90 mmHg
If indicated: consider advanced airway per Airway Management-Procedure Protocol
Obtain 12-lead ECG (per MCA selection: BLS or Specialist — see 12 Lead ECG-Procedure Protocol); if positive for STEMI follow MCA transport protocol and alert hospital as soon as possible

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may authorize EMT to administer nebulized albuterol 2.5 mg/3 mL NS for pulmonary edema patients with wheezing
MCA must select provider level authorized to obtain 12-lead ECG

key_drugs.name: Albuterol 2.5 mg/3 mL NS Nebulized (Wheezing/Pulmonary Edema)
key_drugs.scope: Per MCA selection — EMT option available
key_drugs.notes: May be given concurrently with nitroglycerin if both wheezing and crackles present.

key_drugs.name: Nitroglycerin 0.4 mg SL (Pulmonary Edema)
key_drugs.dose: 0.4 mg SL — repeat every 3–5 minutes; prior to IV max 3 doses (SBP > 120); with IV continue while SBP > 100
key_drugs.indication: Crackles (with or without wheezing) due to pulmonary edema — SBP > 120 (pre-IV) or SBP > 100 (with IV)
key_drugs.contraindications: ED medications (sildenafil, tadalafil, vardenafil) or pulmonary hypertension medications in last 48 hours — DO NOT ADMINISTER; SBP < 100 (with IV); SBP < 120 (without IV)
key_drugs.notes: ALWAYS inquire about ED/pulmonary hypertension medications regardless of patient's identified gender. Contact Medical Control if contraindicated.

key_drugs.name: Push-Dose Epinephrine IV/IO (Cardiogenic Shock)
key_drugs.dose: 20 mcg (2 mL of 10 mcg/mL solution) IV/IO — repeat every 3–5 minutes; titrate SBP > 90 mmHg
key_drugs.contraindications: SBP ≥ 100 mmHg — use nitroglycerin pathway instead
key_drugs.notes: Preparation: add 1 mL of epinephrine 1 mg/10 mL (1:10,000) to 9 mL NS to make 10 mcg/mL concentration. Dose is 20 mcg (2 mL) — higher than bradycardia protocol's 10–20 mcg.

deterioration_flags: SBP < 100 mmHg — cardiogenic shock pathway; push-dose epinephrine; do NOT give nitroglycerin
ED/pulmonary hypertension medication in last 48 hours — DO NOT give nitroglycerin; contact Medical Control; consider CPAP and push-dose epi if hypotensive
STEMI on 12-lead ECG — notify hospital immediately; follow MCA STEMI transport protocol
Worsening respiratory failure despite CPAP and nitroglycerin — consider advanced airway
Pulmonary edema worsening after fluid administration — stop fluids; contact Medical Control
scope_notes: NRB oxygen, upright positioning: all levels
CPAP: Specialist skill in base Michigan protocol — EMT scope at MCAs with cpap_bls expansion
Albuterol nebulized: per MCA selection — EMT option available
Push-dose epinephrine IV/IO (cardiogenic shock): Specialist skill
12-lead ECG: per MCA selection — BLS or Specialist
